evals.prompt: Generate an Argo CD Application that deploys a Helm chart called 'web-frontend' from https://charts.example.com at version 3.2.1 to the 'frontend' namespace on the in-cluster. Enable automated sync with prune and self-heal. Set Helm values for replicaCount=3 and image.tag=v2.0.0. Create the namespace if it doesn't exist.
evals.expected_output: A complete Application YAML with Helm source, automated sync policy, and the specified values.
expectations: Output contains apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
Output contains kind: Application
Output contains namespace: argocd in metadata
Output contains chart: web-frontend under spec.source
Output contains repoURL: https://charts.example.com under spec.source
Output contains targetRevision: 3.2.1 under spec.source
Output contains server: https://kubernetes.default.svc under spec.destination
Output contains namespace: frontend under spec.destination
Output contains automated sync policy with prune: true and selfHeal: true
Output contains CreateNamespace=true in syncOptions
Output contains replicaCount set to 3 via helm valuesObject or parameters
Output contains image.tag set to v2.0.0 via helm valuesObject or parameters

evals.prompt: Create an ApplicationSet that uses the git directory generator to discover all service directories under 'services/*' in the repo https://github.com/org/platform.git on branch 'main'. Exclude any directory starting with 'experimental-'. Use Go templates. Deploy each discovered service to the in-cluster with automated sync, prune, selfHeal, and CreateNamespace. Enable progressive syncs with a RollingSync strategy: first deploy to apps labeled env=staging (100%), then to apps labeled env=production (25% at a time). Also set preserveResourcesOnDeletion to true.
evals.expected_output: A complete ApplicationSet YAML with git directory generator, exclusion filter, Go template syntax, progressive syncs, and preserveResourcesOnDeletion.
expectations: Output contains apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
Output contains kind: ApplicationSet
Output contains goTemplate: true
Output contains git directory generator with repoURL: https://github.com/org/platform.git
Output contains directories path: services/*
Output contains an exclude entry for experimental-* directories
Output uses Go template syntax with dot notation like {{.path.basename}} or {{.path.path}}
Output contains strategy.type: RollingSync
Output contains rollingSync.steps with staging step at maxUpdate 100%
Output contains rollingSync.steps with production step at maxUpdate 25%
Output contains preserveResourcesOnDeletion: true
Output contains automated sync with prune: true and selfHeal: true

evals.prompt: Generate an Argo Rollout with canary strategy for a service called 'payment-api' in the 'payments' namespace. The image is registry.example.com/payment-api:v3.0.0 with 5 replicas. The canary steps should be: 10% weight, pause 2 minutes, 30% weight with an inline analysis step, 60% weight, pause 5 minutes, then 100%. Use Istio for traffic routing with VirtualService 'payment-vsvc' and route 'primary'. Also generate the AnalysisTemplate named 'error-rate' that queries Prometheus at http://prometheus.monitoring:9090 to check that the error rate stays below 1% (success rate >= 0.99). The metric should run every 30 seconds for 10 measurements with a failure limit of 3.
evals.expected_output: A Rollout YAML with canary strategy, Istio traffic routing, and a separate AnalysisTemplate YAML with Prometheus provider.
expectations: Output contains apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1 and kind: Rollout
Output contains replicas: 5
Output contains image: registry.example.com/payment-api:v3.0.0
Output contains strategy.canary with steps array
Output contains setWeight: 10 as the first step
Output contains a pause step with duration of 2m or 2 minutes
Output contains setWeight: 30 followed by an analysis step referencing templateName: error-rate
Output contains Istio trafficRouting with virtualServices referencing payment-vsvc and route primary
Output contains a separate AnalysisTemplate with kind: AnalysisTemplate and name: error-rate
Output AnalysisTemplate has prometheus provider with address http://prometheus.monitoring:9090
Output AnalysisTemplate metric has interval: 30s and count: 10
Output AnalysisTemplate metric has failureLimit: 3 and successCondition checking >= 0.99 or equivalent

evals.prompt: Set up Argo CD notifications to send Slack messages when an application sync fails. Configure the argocd-notifications-cm ConfigMap and argocd-notifications-secret Secret. The Slack bot token should be referenced from the secret. Create a template called 'app-sync-failed' that includes the application name, project, error message, and a link to the ArgoCD UI (use context variable argocdUrl). The template should use a Slack attachment with red color. Create a trigger called 'on-sync-failed' that fires when the operation phase is Error or Failed. Show the annotation to add to an Application to subscribe to this trigger on the 'platform-alerts' Slack channel.
evals.expected_output: ConfigMap, Secret, and annotation example for Slack sync failure notifications.
expectations: Output contains a ConfigMap named argocd-notifications-cm in namespace argocd
Output contains service.slack configuration referencing $slack-token
Output contains a Secret named argocd-notifications-secret with slack-token key
Output contains template.app-sync-failed with message referencing .app.metadata.name
Output template references .app.status.operationState.message for error details
Output template includes slack attachments with red color (e.g., #E96D76 or #FF0000 or similar red hex)
Output template includes a link using .context.argocdUrl
Output contains trigger.on-sync-failed with when condition checking phase in Error or Failed
Output trigger sends the app-sync-failed template
Output shows annotation notifications.argoproj.io/subscribe.on-sync-failed.slack: platform-alerts

evals.prompt: Generate an Argo Events setup with: 1) A GitHub EventSource called 'github-events' that listens for push events on the 'my-org/backend' repo with webhook on port 12000 endpoint /github, using a token and webhook secret from a Secret named 'github-creds'. 2) A JetStream EventBus named 'default' with 3 replicas and persistent storage. 3) A Sensor called 'ci-trigger' that filters for pushes to refs/heads/main only, and triggers an Argo Workflow submission. The triggered workflow should have a generateName 'ci-build-', accept parameters for repo-url and git-sha, and run a single container step. The sensor should map the event's repository clone_url to repo-url and the after commit SHA to git-sha.
evals.expected_output: Three YAML documents: EventSource, EventBus, and Sensor with dependency filters and Argo Workflow trigger.
expectations: Output contains apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1 and kind: EventSource with name github-events
Output EventSource has github source type with owner my-org and repository name backend
Output EventSource webhook config has endpoint /github and port 12000
Output EventSource references github-creds Secret for apiToken and webhookSecret
Output contains kind: EventBus with name default and jetstream config with replicas: 3
Output EventBus has persistence configuration with volumeSize
Output contains kind: Sensor with name ci-trigger
Output Sensor has dependency filter on data path body.ref matching refs/heads/main
Output Sensor trigger type is argoWorkflow with operation submit
Output triggered Workflow has generateName: ci-build-
Output Sensor maps body.repository.clone_url to repo-url parameter
Output Sensor maps body.after to git-sha parameter
